groups.label: Preflight
groups.description: Confirm ASM-Lite, canonical scene access, and the Unity test host are ready before any setup, lifecycle, or runtime suites run.

suites.label: ASM-Lite Readiness Check
suites.description: Make sure ASM-Lite, its prefab, the smoke checklist, canonical scene acquisition, and the Unity test host are ready before running setup or lifecycle suites.
suites.expectedOutcome: ASM-Lite opens, the package prefab is available, the smoke checklist loads, the canonical scene can be reacquired from safe editor states, and the Unity test host is ready.
suites.debugHint: If this check fails, confirm the package is installed in the external UAT project, Assets/Click ME.unity exists, and the smoke checklist path resolves.

cases.label: Open ASM-Lite
cases.description: Open ASM-Lite from Unity so the overlay can confirm the editor window is available.
cases.expectedOutcome: ASM-Lite opens and is ready for automation.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, check that the ASM-Lite editor window can open from Unity and that editor logs do not show startup errors.

steps.label: ASM-Lite is open
steps.description: Open the ASM-Lite editor window.
steps.expectedOutcome: ASM-Lite opens and is ready for automation.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, check that the ASM-Lite editor window can open from Unity and that editor logs do not show startup errors.

cases.label: Package files are available
cases.description: Check that the installed ASM-Lite package includes the prefab needed by later suites.
cases.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite prefab is found inside the installed package.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, check that the ASM-Lite package is installed and Unity imported its prefab files.

steps.label: ASM-Lite prefab is available
steps.description: Find the ASM-Lite prefab inside the installed package.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite prefab is found inside the installed package.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, check that the ASM-Lite package is installed and Unity imported its prefab files.

cases.label: Smoke checklist loads
cases.description: Check that the overlay can read the smoke-test checklist it uses to show suites and steps.
cases.expectedOutcome: The overlay can load the smoke-test checklist.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, check that Tools/ci/smoke/suite-catalog.json is valid JSON and matches the expected schema.

steps.label: Smoke checklist is available
steps.description: Load the smoke-test checklist used by the overlay.
steps.expectedOutcome: The overlay can load the smoke-test checklist.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, check that Tools/ci/smoke/suite-catalog.json is valid JSON and matches the expected schema.

cases.label: Test host is ready
cases.description: Check that the Unity test host is ready to receive overlay commands.
cases.expectedOutcome: The Unity test host is ready to receive overlay commands.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, check the smoke session folder, host startup logs, and command/event file paths.

steps.label: Test host is ready
steps.description: Confirm the Unity test host is ready to receive overlay commands.
steps.expectedOutcome: The Unity test host is ready to receive overlay commands.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, check the smoke session folder, host startup logs, and command/event file paths.

cases.label: Canonical scene already open
cases.description: Open the canonical scene when it is already the target scene.
cases.expectedOutcome: The canonical scene remains active and setup can continue.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect active-scene path normalization.

steps.label: Canonical scene is open
steps.description: Open Assets/Click ME.unity.
steps.debugHint: If the scene does not load, confirm the fixture scene exists.

cases.label: Temp unsaved scene open
cases.description: Create a temporary unsaved setup scene state, then reacquire the canonical scene.
cases.expectedOutcome: The host returns to Assets/Click ME.unity from a temporary scene state.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect fixture mutation cleanup and scene restore diagnostics.

steps.label: Canonical scene is reopened
steps.description: Apply the temporary scene setup mutation and open Assets/Click ME.unity.
steps.expectedOutcome: Assets/Click ME.unity is active after temporary scene setup.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect temp scene mutation cleanup.

groups.label: Setup
groups.description: Open the canonical scene, target the canonical avatar, and prepare the default ASM-Lite prefab scaffold in one operator-controlled setup suite.

suites.label: Setup Scene / Avatar / Prefab
suites.description: Open the canonical scene, prove ASM-Lite can open from closed state and reopen after a close, select Oct25_Dress, add the default prefab scaffold, and confirm the rebuild action is available when the operator runs this setup suite.
suites.expectedOutcome: The canonical scene is loaded, ASM-Lite opens from closed state and reopens cleanly after a close, Oct25_Dress is targeted, the prefab scaffold is present, and the rebuild action is visible without running lifecycle steps.
suites.debugHint: Run this combined setup suite before lifecycle or runtime suites when Unity is not already on the canonical scene/avatar/prefab context or when the ASM-Lite window lifecycle needs a quick smoke check.

cases.label: Scene, window, avatar, and prefab setup
cases.description: Load the canonical scene, prove the ASM-Lite window opens from a closed state and reopens cleanly after a close, then establish the editor avatar context and prefab scaffold.
cases.expectedOutcome: Assets/Click ME.unity is loaded, the ASM-Lite window survives close/open and reopen-after-close checks, ASM-Lite is attached to Oct25_Dress, and the primary rebuild action is visible.
cases.debugHint: If setup stalls, verify the scene and avatar names match the catalog fixture, inspect ASM-Lite window lifecycle state, and confirm the window is not hidden behind another dock tab.

steps.label: Click ME scene is open
steps.description: Load the canonical smoke-test scene on operator request.
steps.expectedOutcome: Assets/Click ME.unity is the active Unity scene.
steps.debugHint: If the scene does not load, confirm the fixture scene exists at Assets/Click ME.unity.

steps.label: Existing window is closed
steps.description: Close any existing ASM-Lite automation window before the first open check.
steps.expectedOutcome: No existing ASM-Lite automation window remains open.
steps.debugHint: If close fails, inspect Unity editor window lifecycle.

steps.label: ASM-Lite window is open
steps.description: Open the ASM-Lite automation window from a closed state.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window is available and focused.
steps.debugHint: If open fails, inspect menu/window initialization.

steps.label: ASM-Lite window is focused
steps.description: Assert the ASM-Lite automation window has editor focus after opening.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window is the focused editor window.
steps.debugHint: If focus fails, inspect dock/tab state and retry after closing stale windows.

steps.label: ASM-Lite window is closed
steps.description: Close the ASM-Lite automation window before the reopen check.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite automation window is closed.
steps.debugHint: If close fails, inspect stale editor window references.

steps.label: ASM-Lite window is reopened
steps.description: Reopen the ASM-Lite automation window after closing it.
steps.debugHint: If reopen fails, inspect stale editor window references.

steps.label: ASM-Lite window is focused
steps.description: Assert the reopened ASM-Lite automation window has editor focus.
steps.expectedOutcome: The reopened ASM-Lite window is focused.
steps.debugHint: If focus fails, inspect Unity layout state.

steps.label: Oct25_Dress is selected
steps.description: Bind the canonical avatar fixture to the automation window.
steps.expectedOutcome: Oct25_Dress is the active automation target in the ASM-Lite window.
steps.debugHint: If selection fails, confirm the avatar exists in Assets/Click ME.unity and is not hidden or renamed.

steps.label: ASM-Lite scaffold is added
steps.description: Add the ASM-Lite prefab scaffold to the current automation target.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite prefab scaffold is attached to Oct25_Dress and generation-ready.
steps.debugHint: If add-prefab fails, inspect avatar prerequisites and generated asset path state before retrying.

steps.label: Rebuild action is visible
steps.description: Assert that the primary rebuild action is now available in the window.
steps.expectedOutcome: The primary rebuild action is visible, signaling that the scaffold install succeeded.
steps.debugHint: If the primary action does not change, inspect whether the prefab install completed and whether the window refreshed.

suites.label: Avatar Picking Regression Checks
suites.description: Check that the smoke tools pick the avatar you meant to edit, and explain clearly when the target is missing, duplicated, hidden, or not a scene avatar.
suites.expectedOutcome: Avatar selection resolves only deterministic scene avatar targets and reports coded diagnostics for invalid or ambiguous targets.
suites.debugHint: If these cases fail, inspect Unity selection state, fixture mutation cleanup, prefab asset persistence, and avatar-name normalization before deeper setup suites.

cases.label: Use the avatar I already picked
cases.description: Use the scene avatar that is already selected before the smoke tool starts picking a target.
cases.expectedOutcome: The selected canonical avatar is used as the automation target.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-scene-avatar precedence.

steps.label: Selected avatar is used
steps.description: Use the scene avatar that is already selected before the smoke tool starts picking a target.
steps.expectedOutcome: The selected canonical avatar is used as the automation target.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-scene-avatar precedence.

cases.label: Find the main avatar by name
cases.description: With nothing selected, find the expected scene avatar by name.
cases.expectedOutcome: The active scene avatar matching the fixture name is selected.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect avatar name normalization and active scene object discovery.

steps.label: Avatar is found by name
steps.description: With nothing selected, find the expected scene avatar by name.
steps.expectedOutcome: The active scene avatar matching the fixture name is selected.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect avatar name normalization and active scene object discovery.

cases.label: Tell me I picked the wrong object
cases.description: If the current selection is not an avatar, stop and explain that clearly.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_SELECTED_OBJECT_NOT_AVATAR is reported without mutating a prefab asset.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected object type checks before name lookup.

steps.label: Non-avatar selection is reported
steps.description: If the current selection is not an avatar, stop and explain that clearly.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_SELECTED_OBJECT_NOT_AVATAR is reported without mutating a prefab asset.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected object type checks before name lookup.

args.expectedDiagnosticContains: selected object is not a valid avatar

cases.label: Respect the avatar I picked
cases.description: If I deliberately pick a different avatar, use that one instead of guessing.
cases.expectedOutcome: The selected avatar wins over fixture-name lookup deterministically.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-avatar precedence and wrong-avatar fixture setup.

steps.label: Selected alternate avatar is used
steps.description: If I deliberately pick a different avatar, use that one instead of guessing.
steps.expectedOutcome: The selected avatar wins over fixture-name lookup deterministically.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-avatar precedence and wrong-avatar fixture setup.

cases.label: Allow the avatar I picked even if it is hidden
cases.description: If I explicitly picked a hidden avatar, keep using my choice.
cases.expectedOutcome: The explicitly selected inactive scene avatar is accepted.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-object policy versus active-name lookup policy.

steps.label: Selected inactive avatar is used
steps.description: If I explicitly picked a hidden avatar, keep using my choice.
steps.expectedOutcome: The explicitly selected inactive scene avatar is accepted.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-object policy versus active-name lookup policy.

cases.label: Do not auto-pick a hidden avatar
cases.description: If a hidden avatar is not selected, do not grab it automatically.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported because inactive unselected avatars are not surprise-selected.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect activeInHierarchy filtering in avatar name lookup.

steps.label: Unselected inactive avatar is not used
steps.description: If a hidden avatar is not selected, do not grab it automatically.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported because inactive unselected avatars are not surprise-selected.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect activeInHierarchy filtering in avatar name lookup.

cases.label: Ask me to choose when two avatars match
cases.description: If two live avatars share the same name, stop and ask for a clearer pick.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_AMBIGUOUS is reported until the operator selects a target.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect duplicate-name counting and selection cleanup.

steps.label: Duplicate avatar names are reported
steps.description: If two live avatars share the same name, stop and ask for a clearer pick.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_AMBIGUOUS is reported until the operator selects a target.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect duplicate-name counting and selection cleanup.

cases.label: Use my pick when names clash
cases.description: If names clash but I already selected one avatar, use my chosen target.
cases.expectedOutcome: The selected canonical avatar disambiguates duplicate names.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-avatar precedence over ambiguous name lookup.

steps.label: Selected avatar resolves duplicate names
steps.description: If names clash but I already selected one avatar, use my chosen target.
steps.expectedOutcome: The selected canonical avatar disambiguates duplicate names.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-avatar precedence over ambiguous name lookup.

cases.label: Reject a prefab from the Project window
cases.description: If I selected a prefab asset instead of a scene avatar, reject it clearly.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_PREFAB_ASSET is reported because prefab assets are not scene avatar instances.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect EditorUtility.IsPersistent checks before scene object selection.

steps.label: Prefab asset selection is reported
steps.description: If I selected a prefab asset instead of a scene avatar, reject it clearly.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_PREFAB_ASSET is reported because prefab assets are not scene avatar instances.

cases.label: Explain when the avatar is missing
cases.description: If the expected avatar is gone, explain that clearly instead of guessing.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported for the missing fixture avatar.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selection cleanup and avatarName overrides.

steps.label: Missing avatar is reported
steps.description: If the expected avatar is gone, explain that clearly instead of guessing.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported for the missing fixture avatar.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selection cleanup and avatarName overrides.

cases.label: Ignore same-name objects that are not avatars
cases.description: Ignore lookalike scene objects that share the name but are not real avatars.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported because non-avatar objects are ignored during name lookup.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect descriptor filtering during name lookup.

steps.label: Same-name non-avatar objects are ignored
steps.description: Ignore lookalike scene objects that share the name but are not real avatars.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported because non-avatar objects are ignored during name lookup.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect descriptor filtering during name lookup.

groups.label: Lifecycle
groups.description: Exercise the package-managed lifecycle actions that rewire or detach ASM-Lite content.

suites.label: Rebuild / Vendorize / Detach / Return
suites.description: Run the deterministic lifecycle roundtrip from rebuild through package-managed return.
suites.expectedOutcome: Lifecycle actions complete in order and the avatar returns to a clean package-managed ASM-Lite state.
suites.debugHint: If the roundtrip drifts, inspect generated asset ownership and whether the avatar still points at vendorized content.

cases.label: Package-managed roundtrip
cases.description: Confirm lifecycle actions can mutate state and return to the package-managed baseline.
cases.expectedOutcome: The lifecycle roundtrip completes without leaving the avatar in a detached or stale vendorized state.
cases.debugHint: Use generated asset paths and install-path diagnostics to isolate which lifecycle transition drifted.

steps.label: ASM-Lite assets are rebuilt
steps.description: Rebuild generated ASM-Lite assets for the canonical avatar.
steps.expectedOutcome: Generated assets rebuild successfully and reflect the latest package-managed source.
steps.debugHint: If rebuild fails, inspect generated asset write locations and the selected avatar binding.

steps.label: Post-rebuild cleanup is applied
steps.description: Reset known ASM-Lite lifecycle drift before the next lifecycle action.
steps.expectedOutcome: Known ASM-Lite generated/vendorized state is returned to a package-managed baseline.
steps.debugHint: If hygiene cleanup fails, inspect package-managed restore and known generated asset paths before continuing.

steps.label: Generated assets are vendorized
steps.description: Copy generated assets under Assets/ASM-Lite for vendorized workflow coverage.
steps.expectedOutcome: Vendorized generated assets exist and the avatar now points at the vendorized copy.
steps.debugHint: If vendorize fails, inspect whether the generated asset target path is writable and deterministic.

steps.label: Post-vendorize cleanup is applied
steps.description: Return known ASM-Lite vendorized state to package-managed baseline before detach coverage.
steps.expectedOutcome: Vendorized references and known generated state are cleaned back to package-managed baseline.
steps.debugHint: If hygiene cleanup fails, preserve the state for review and inspect stale vendorized references.

steps.label: ASM-Lite component is detached
steps.description: Detach the editable ASM-Lite component while preserving the baked content.
steps.expectedOutcome: The avatar is detached from the editable component without breaking the generated state.
steps.debugHint: If detach leaves a broken avatar, inspect component cleanup and generated controller references.

steps.label: Post-detach cleanup is applied
steps.description: Restore the detached avatar to package-managed baseline before final return verification.
steps.expectedOutcome: Detached state is recovered and known ASM-Lite generated state is package-managed again.
steps.debugHint: If hygiene cleanup fails, preserve the state for review and inspect detached recovery diagnostics.

steps.label: Package-managed state is restored
steps.description: Reattach the avatar to package-managed ASM-Lite assets and clear vendorized drift.
steps.expectedOutcome: The avatar returns to a package-managed ASM-Lite state with no lingering vendorized dependency.
steps.debugHint: If return-to-package-managed fails, inspect whether detach or vendorize left stale asset references behind.

groups.label: Playmode / Runtime
groups.description: Validate the runtime-facing ASM-Lite path inside a playmode transition without restarting the host session.

suites.label: Enter / Validate / Exit Playmode
suites.description: Prepare the canonical avatar, enter playmode, validate runtime state, and return to edit mode.
suites.expectedOutcome: The canonical avatar enters playmode with valid runtime ASM-Lite state and exits back to edit mode cleanly.
suites.debugHint: If runtime validation fails, inspect whether the prefab scaffold was present before entering playmode and whether playmode exited cleanly.

cases.label: Runtime validation
cases.description: Prepare the avatar for runtime checks and verify the runtime component stays valid across the playmode boundary.
cases.expectedOutcome: ASM-Lite playmode preprocessing completes and returns to edit mode without requiring a new Unity session.
cases.debugHint: If playmode hangs or exits dirty, inspect editor scaffold setup, VRCFury/NDMF preprocessing, and playmode state transitions before rerunning.

steps.label: ASM-Lite window is open
steps.description: Open the product window before runtime setup begins.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window is available for runtime preparation.
steps.debugHint: If the window is missing, inspect editor window lifecycle before entering playmode.

steps.label: Oct25_Dress is selected
steps.description: Target the canonical avatar fixture before runtime preparation.
steps.expectedOutcome: Oct25_Dress is the active runtime-preparation target.
steps.debugHint: If selection fails, confirm the canonical avatar still exists in the loaded scene.

steps.label: ASM-Lite scaffold is present
steps.description: Add the ASM-Lite prefab scaffold if the avatar is not already prepared for runtime checks.
steps.expectedOutcome: The avatar has the ASM-Lite scaffold required for runtime validation.
steps.debugHint: If scaffold setup fails, inspect editor-side generation prerequisites before entering playmode.

steps.label: Play mode is entered
steps.description: Transition the Unity host into playmode for runtime coverage.
steps.expectedOutcome: The host enters playmode and begins runtime validation on the canonical avatar.
steps.debugHint: If enter-playmode hangs, inspect unsaved scene prompts or compilation state before retrying.

steps.label: Runtime component is valid
steps.description: Assert that ASM-Lite survives playmode preprocessing on the prepared avatar.
steps.expectedOutcome: The prepared avatar is valid after playmode preprocessing; editor-only ASM-Lite component stripping is accepted.
steps.debugHint: If runtime validation fails, inspect generated parameter/controller state, VRCFury/NDMF preprocessing, and avatar lookup diagnostics.

steps.label: Play mode is exited
steps.description: Return the host to edit mode after runtime validation completes.
steps.expectedOutcome: The host exits playmode cleanly and returns to edit mode without restarting Unity.
steps.debugHint: If exit-playmode fails, inspect playmode teardown logs and pending scene state.
